addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by examining the function and its context.

content.content:
1	- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
2	- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
3	- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
4	- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
5	- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
6	- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
7

file.content:
- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts

content.text: I'll read the relevant memory files and the function body.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub
3	description: zopfli.c hard-codes an x86_64 FILE.h include; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc on this aarch64 host
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
8	---
9	
10	`Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` line 7 hard-codes `#include <x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h>`, but this host is aarch64 (only `/usr/include/aarch64-linux-gnu/...` exists). goto-cc fails to find the header.
11	
12	Fix (no C change): a stub dir already exists at `/tmp/cbmc-inc/x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h`. Always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` to `run-cbmc` when verifying functions in this file. If it's gone, recreate it pointing the x86_64 path at the aarch64 FILE.h. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].
13

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub
description: zopfli.c hard-codes an x86_64 FILE.h include; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc on this aarch64 host
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
---

`Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` line 7 hard-codes `#include <x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h>`, but this host is aarch64 (only `/usr/include/aarch64-linux-gnu/...` exists). goto-cc fails to find the header.

Fix (no C change): a stub dir already exists at `/tmp/cbmc-inc/x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h`. Always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` to `run-cbmc` when verifying functions in this file. If it's gone, recreate it pointing the x86_64 path at the aarch64 FILE.h. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity
3	description: Why functions with several is_fresh pointer params verify vacuously (0 mutation kills) under run-cbmc
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
8	---
9	
10	`run-cbmc` runs CBMC with a fixed `--depth 200` (`_CBMC_DEPTH` in `tools/run_cbmc_and_mutation_testing.py`, not env-configurable; do not hard-code depth in specs).
11	
12	In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` (which `#include`s `<stdlib.h>`), each `__CPROVER_is_fresh(...)` in a `requires` clause routes through CBMC's full `malloc` model (memory-map disjointness, leak tracking, should-fail check) — ~70 GOTO steps per fresh object. A function needing valid memory for ~4 pointer params (e.g. `AddBit(int bit, unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)`) builds a harness of ~280–300 steps, which EXCEEDS depth 200 before the body runs.
13	
14	Effect: CBMC truncates the path, the postconditions (checked at function exit) are never reached, so they pass vacuously. The tool reports "verified successfully" but it is a vacuous proof — and mutation testing then kills 0 mutants regardless of how strong the spec is. Symptom to recognize: adding `__CPROVER_ensures(1 == 0)` still "verifies" (run via direct cbmc and grep `postcondition.*SUCCESS`; raise `--depth` to ~350 to see it flip to FAILURE).
15	
16	Without `<stdlib.h>` the same 4-is_fresh harness fits in depth 200 (light built-in alloc) — so it's the malloc model, not is_fresh per se. `rw_ok` for scalars is lighter but unsound for written-through out-params. No sound formulation fits depth 200 here.
17	
18	Confirmed instances: `AddBit`, `AddLZ77Data`, `BoundaryPMFinal`, `BoundaryPM`, and `ExtractBitLengths` (recursive; params `Node*(*lists)[2], leaves, pool` — its self-call + `InitNode` are both replaced by contracts, so the contract must be inductive). For `BoundaryPM` a requires(is_fresh pool/lists/leaves)+assigns(object_whole(lists),pool->next) contract verifies at depth 200 but is vacuous (ensures(1==0) SUCCEEDS at 200, FAILS at 400). Extra obstacle: any `__CPROVER_ensures` that dereferences a nested list pointer (e.g. `__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count)`) fails NON-vacuously — the entry-time snapshot derefs the nondeterministic `lists[i][j]` (R_OK/bounds FAILURE at the body's first `lists[index][1]->count`). Only scalar facts like `pool->next >= __CPROVER_old(pool->next)` are safely addable. Net: kill score 0/27 is tooling-forced, not spec weakness. `BoundaryPMFinal` (same `Node*(*lists)[2],leaves,pool` shape, plus nested `is_fresh` on `lists[index][1]`, `lists[index-1][0/1]`, `pool->next` so even heavier harness) verifies a STRONG branch-conditional `ensures` (deref nested list pointers — safe here because those nested pointers are made fresh in requires), but `ensures(0==1)` still SUCCEEDS at depth 200 AND 450 → vacuous, 0/16 kills forced.
19	
20	`ExtractBitLengths(Node *chain, Node *leaves, unsigned *bitlengths)` (3 indexed pointer params; restricted to single-node chain via `requires chain->tail==0` so the `chain->tail` walk is safe without per-node freshness) directly demonstrates the wall both ways: (1) `is_fresh` on all 3 → "verified" but VACUOUS (0/12 kills, loop-body mutants at lines 332-340 all survive). (2) Swapping the two *indexed* pointers (`leaves`, `bitlengths`) to the light `r_ok`/`w_ok` predicates DOES fit depth 200 and REACHES the body (non-vacuous) — but then CBMC cannot discharge the array bounds / `assigns`-assignable checks: `r_ok`/`w_ok`, even combined with `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(p)==n` and `__CPROVER_POINTER_OFFSET(p)==0` assumptions, do NOT make the bounds checker treat the object as sized — `bitlengths[leaves[k].count]` reports "pointer outside object bounds" + "is assignable: FAILURE". Only `is_fresh` establishes a bounded object for write-through/indexed accesses, and only `is_fresh` is heavy enough to overrun depth 200. So: indexed pointers MUST be `is_fresh` (sound) → vacuous; or light predicates (fits) → unsound bounds FAILURE. No middle ground. Net 0/12 is tooling-forced.
21	
22	`AddLZ77Data(lz77, lstart, lend, expected_data_size, ll_symbols, ll_lengths, d_symbols, d_lengths, bp, out, outsize)` is the heaviest seen: ~11 pointers needing memory (`lz77` + its `litlens`/`dists` arrays, four `ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`/`ZOPFLI_NUM_D` tables, `bp`, `outsize`, `out`, `*out`). A sound strong contract (is_fresh on all, forall bounding litlens/dists per literal-vs-match, ll/d_lengths in [1,15], expected_data_size==0) verifies at depth 200 but 0/40 kills — even `i<lend`→`i>lend` and the literal/match `dist==0`→`dist!=0` branch mutants survive, proving the loop body is never reached. Compounding the vacuity, it is also non-vacuously UNVERIFIABLE if reached: the callees `AddHuffmanBits`/`AddBits` are contract-replaced and their `ensures` constrain only `*bp`/`*outsize` (not buffer validity), so after the first call `*out` is havoc'd and the second sequential call's `requires is_fresh(*out, *outsize+1)` cannot be discharged. So lightening the harness to fit depth 200 would flip it to FAILURE, not kills. Net 0/40 tooling-forced.
23	
24	`AddNonCompressedBlock(options, final, in, instart, inend, bp, out, outsize)` (4 pointer params needing memory: `in`, `bp`, `out`, `*out`, `outsize`; `options` is only `(void)`-cast so never deref'd, no is_fresh needed) is the same wall. A general contract (is_fresh all, `*bp<=7`, `*outsize` in [1, max_malloc/2], `inend-instart<=8`, ensures `*bp==0` and `*outsize>=old+4`) verifies at depth 200 → 0/26 kills. I also tried the OPPOSITE extreme to force reachability: minimal harness `*outsize==1` + `*bp==0` + `inend-instart<=1` (so the in-body `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` memset stays 1-2 bytes, ruling out "large memset blows depth" as the cause) PLUS byte-precise postconditions pinning every emitted byte ((*out)[1]=BFINAL, [2..5]=LEN/NLEN header, [6]=data). Still 0/26 — so it is the is_fresh/malloc *harness setup* exhausting depth 200, not the loop body's memset size. No spec change rescues kills here.
25	
26	`CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall(ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lstart, lend)` (same lz77+litlens/dists+ll/d_lengths shape as `AddLZ77Data`, 4 indexed pointers + lz77 struct deref = 5 is_fresh objects) is the same wall. A sound strong contract (is_fresh on all 5, `lend<=lz77->size`, forall bounding `litlens[i]<=258` and `dists[i]!=0 ==> litlens[i]>=3 && dists[i]<=32768` for callee preconds, `assigns()`, ensures `ret>=ll_lengths[256]` and `lstart==lend ==> ret==ll_lengths[256]`) verifies at depth 200 → 0/16 kills, all 16 surviving mutants are loop-guard/body RELATIONAL ops (never reached). Confirmed vacuous: added `ensures(1==0)` → SUCCESS at depth 200, but postcondition.3 FAILS at depth 600 (body finally reached). Net 0/16 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.
27	
28	`CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts(ll_counts, d_counts, ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lstart, lend)` is the HEAVIEST count-table case: its `if` branch delegates to `CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall` (so needs the full lz77+litlens+dists+forall-bounds harness) AND its `else` branch sums over four full histogram tables (so needs is_fresh on ll_counts/d_counts/ll_lengths/d_lengths too) = 7 is_fresh objects. A sound strong contract (is_fresh all 7, lstart<=lend<=lz77->size, forall litlens<=258 & dists DEFLATE bounds, assigns(), ensures ret>=ll_lengths[256]) verifies at depth 200 → 0/21 kills, all 21 surviving mutants are the if-guard relational/arith ops at line 317 and the loop bodies (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via the documented `ensures(1==0)` trick: it still "verifies" (mutation testing only runs post-success). Net 0/21 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.
29	
30	`TryOptimizeHuffmanForRle(lz77, lstart, lend, ll_counts, d_counts, ll_lengths, d_lengths)` is the same wall — it calls `CalculateTreeSize`, `CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts` (twice), `OptimizeHuffmanForRle`, `ZopfliCalculateBitLengths`, `PatchDistanceCodesForBuggyDecoders`, all contract-replaced, so its requires is the UNION of theirs: is_fresh on lz77 + nested litlens/dists + ll_counts/d_counts/ll_lengths/d_lengths (7 is_fresh objects), forall litlens<=258 & DEFLATE dist bounds, ll/d_lengths<=15, counts<2^(64-9), assigns(object_whole(ll_lengths),object_whole(d_lengths)), ensures ret>=26. Verifies at depth 200 → 0/9 kills; all 9 survivors are the line-1272 min-select comparison and the two `treesize+datasize` return sums (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via `ensures(ret>=100000000)` → still SUCCESS at depth 200. Two extra gotchas while building it: (1) the quantified `requires` deref `litlens[qi]` generates a well-definedness BOUNDS assertion at the *caller* even though it's only assumed in the callee — guard the quantifier with `qi < lz77->size`; (2) unconstrained `lz77->size` makes the is_fresh byte-count `size * sizeof(*litlens)` overflow size_t (counterexample: size≈2^63), so add `requires(lz77->size <= ((size_t)-1)/sizeof(*lz77->litlens))`. Net 0/9 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.
31	
32	`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt(lz77, lpos, ll_counts, d_counts)` is the same wall — it needs 8 is_fresh objects: `lz77` struct + its nested `ll_counts`/`d_counts`/`ll_symbol`/`d_symbol`/`dists` arrays + the two output tables `ll_counts`/`d_counts`. A sound strong contract verifies at depth 200 → 0/41 kills, all 41 survivors are the cumulative-index arithmetic (`llpos+i`, `lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`) and the decrement loops (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via the `ensures(1==0)` trick → still SUCCESS at depth 200. Two sizing details worth keeping: the cumulative arrays are indexed only up to the end of lpos's chunk, so size them exactly `(ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*(lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)` / `(ZOPFLI_NUM_D*(lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_D)+ZOPFLI_NUM_D)` elements; and the symbol arrays are read at indices `lz77->ll_symbol[i]`/`d_symbol[i]` used to index the output tables, so a `forall (lpos<qi<size) ==> symbol[qi] < ZOPFLI_NUM_*` requires-bound is needed (guard the quantifier with `qi<size`). Net 0/41 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.
33	
34	`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram(lz77, lstart, lend, ll_counts, d_counts)` (the caller of `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt`) is the same wall — 8 is_fresh objects (lz77 struct + nested ll_counts/d_counts/ll_symbol/d_symbol/dists + the two output tables). Its small-range branch counts symbols directly; its large-range branch calls the contract-replaced `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt` (whose contract has NO ensures, so the output is fully havoc'd → no output postcondition can hold there anyway). Sound strong contract: is_fresh all 8 (size the cumulative arrays `ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*(size/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` so they cover every lpos<=size-1 without size-1 underflow when size=0), `lstart<=lend<=lz77->size`, overflow guard `size <= ((size_t)-1)/sizeof(*ll_counts) - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`, `forall qi<size ==> ll_symbol[qi]<ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` and same for d_symbol (full-range, since the callee needs (lpos,size)), assigns(object_whole(ll_counts/d_counts)). Verifies at depth 200 → 0/29 kills; all 29 survivors are the branch-guard (line 1371), the direct-count loop guard/dist-test (1375/1378), the `lstart>0` guard (1387), the subtraction-loop guards (1393/1397) and the `lend-1`/`lstart-1` callee args (1386/1391). Confirmed vacuous via the documented probe: `ensures((lstart+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3>lend) ==> d_counts[0]==999999)` (a false branch-1 claim) still SUCCEEDS at depth 200. Also note loop contracts are USELESS here: the avocado pipeline runs `goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5` WITHOUT `--apply-loop-contracts`, so `__CPROVER_loop_invariant`/loop-`assigns` annotations are silently ignored (loops are just unrolled 5x). Net 0/29 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place. See also [[cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation]].
35	
36	Bonus: at depth >=350, CBMC finds a real latent bug — `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` (macro in zopfli.h) writes through `realloc`'s return with no NULL check. See [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] and [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]].
37

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity
description: Why functions with several is_fresh pointer params verify vacuously (0 mutation kills) under run-cbmc
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
---

`run-cbmc` runs CBMC with a fixed `--depth 200` (`_CBMC_DEPTH` in `tools/run_cbmc_and_mutation_testing.py`, not env-configurable; do not hard-code depth in specs).

In `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` (which `#include`s `<stdlib.h>`), each `__CPROVER_is_fresh(...)` in a `requires` clause routes through CBMC's full `malloc` model (memory-map disjointness, leak tracking, should-fail check) — ~70 GOTO steps per fresh object. A function needing valid memory for ~4 pointer params (e.g. `AddBit(int bit, unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)`) builds a harness of ~280–300 steps, which EXCEEDS depth 200 before the body runs.

Effect: CBMC truncates the path, the postconditions (checked at function exit) are never reached, so they pass vacuously. The tool reports "verified successfully" but it is a vacuous proof — and mutation testing then kills 0 mutants regardless of how strong the spec is. Symptom to recognize: adding `__CPROVER_ensures(1 == 0)` still "verifies" (run via direct cbmc and grep `postcondition.*SUCCESS`; raise `--depth` to ~350 to see it flip to FAILURE).

Without `<stdlib.h>` the same 4-is_fresh harness fits in depth 200 (light built-in alloc) — so it's the malloc model, not is_fresh per se. `rw_ok` for scalars is lighter but unsound for written-through out-params. No sound formulation fits depth 200 here.

Confirmed instances: `AddBit`, `AddLZ77Data`, `BoundaryPMFinal`, `BoundaryPM`, and `ExtractBitLengths` (recursive; params `Node*(*lists)[2], leaves, pool` — its self-call + `InitNode` are both replaced by contracts, so the contract must be inductive). For `BoundaryPM` a requires(is_fresh pool/lists/leaves)+assigns(object_whole(lists),pool->next) contract verifies at depth 200 but is vacuous (ensures(1==0) SUCCEEDS at 200, FAILS at 400). Extra obstacle: any `__CPROVER_ensures` that dereferences a nested list pointer (e.g. `__CPROVER_old(lists[index][1]->count)`) fails NON-vacuously — the entry-time snapshot derefs the nondeterministic `lists[i][j]` (R_OK/bounds FAILURE at the body's first `lists[index][1]->count`). Only scalar facts like `pool->next >= __CPROVER_old(pool->next)` are safely addable. Net: kill score 0/27 is tooling-forced, not spec weakness. `BoundaryPMFinal` (same `Node*(*lists)[2],leaves,pool` shape, plus nested `is_fresh` on `lists[index][1]`, `lists[index-1][0/1]`, `pool->next` so even heavier harness) verifies a STRONG branch-conditional `ensures` (deref nested list pointers — safe here because those nested pointers are made fresh in requires), but `ensures(0==1)` still SUCCEEDS at depth 200 AND 450 → vacuous, 0/16 kills forced.

`ExtractBitLengths(Node *chain, Node *leaves, unsigned *bitlengths)` (3 indexed pointer params; restricted to single-node chain via `requires chain->tail==0` so the `chain->tail` walk is safe without per-node freshness) directly demonstrates the wall both ways: (1) `is_fresh` on all 3 → "verified" but VACUOUS (0/12 kills, loop-body mutants at lines 332-340 all survive). (2) Swapping the two *indexed* pointers (`leaves`, `bitlengths`) to the light `r_ok`/`w_ok` predicates DOES fit depth 200 and REACHES the body (non-vacuous) — but then CBMC cannot discharge the array bounds / `assigns`-assignable checks: `r_ok`/`w_ok`, even combined with `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(p)==n` and `__CPROVER_POINTER_OFFSET(p)==0` assumptions, do NOT make the bounds checker treat the object as sized — `bitlengths[leaves[k].count]` reports "pointer outside object bounds" + "is assignable: FAILURE". Only `is_fresh` establishes a bounded object for write-through/indexed accesses, and only `is_fresh` is heavy enough to overrun depth 200. So: indexed pointers MUST be `is_fresh` (sound) → vacuous; or light predicates (fits) → unsound bounds FAILURE. No middle ground. Net 0/12 is tooling-forced.

`AddLZ77Data(lz77, lstart, lend, expected_data_size, ll_symbols, ll_lengths, d_symbols, d_lengths, bp, out, outsize)` is the heaviest seen: ~11 pointers needing memory (`lz77` + its `litlens`/`dists` arrays, four `ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`/`ZOPFLI_NUM_D` tables, `bp`, `outsize`, `out`, `*out`). A sound strong contract (is_fresh on all, forall bounding litlens/dists per literal-vs-match, ll/d_lengths in [1,15], expected_data_size==0) verifies at depth 200 but 0/40 kills — even `i<lend`→`i>lend` and the literal/match `dist==0`→`dist!=0` branch mutants survive, proving the loop body is never reached. Compounding the vacuity, it is also non-vacuously UNVERIFIABLE if reached: the callees `AddHuffmanBits`/`AddBits` are contract-replaced and their `ensures` constrain only `*bp`/`*outsize` (not buffer validity), so after the first call `*out` is havoc'd and the second sequential call's `requires is_fresh(*out, *outsize+1)` cannot be discharged. So lightening the harness to fit depth 200 would flip it to FAILURE, not kills. Net 0/40 tooling-forced.

`AddNonCompressedBlock(options, final, in, instart, inend, bp, out, outsize)` (4 pointer params needing memory: `in`, `bp`, `out`, `*out`, `outsize`; `options` is only `(void)`-cast so never deref'd, no is_fresh needed) is the same wall. A general contract (is_fresh all, `*bp<=7`, `*outsize` in [1, max_malloc/2], `inend-instart<=8`, ensures `*bp==0` and `*outsize>=old+4`) verifies at depth 200 → 0/26 kills. I also tried the OPPOSITE extreme to force reachability: minimal harness `*outsize==1` + `*bp==0` + `inend-instart<=1` (so the in-body `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` memset stays 1-2 bytes, ruling out "large memset blows depth" as the cause) PLUS byte-precise postconditions pinning every emitted byte ((*out)[1]=BFINAL, [2..5]=LEN/NLEN header, [6]=data). Still 0/26 — so it is the is_fresh/malloc *harness setup* exhausting depth 200, not the loop body's memset size. No spec change rescues kills here.

`CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall(ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lstart, lend)` (same lz77+litlens/dists+ll/d_lengths shape as `AddLZ77Data`, 4 indexed pointers + lz77 struct deref = 5 is_fresh objects) is the same wall. A sound strong contract (is_fresh on all 5, `lend<=lz77->size`, forall bounding `litlens[i]<=258` and `dists[i]!=0 ==> litlens[i]>=3 && dists[i]<=32768` for callee preconds, `assigns()`, ensures `ret>=ll_lengths[256]` and `lstart==lend ==> ret==ll_lengths[256]`) verifies at depth 200 → 0/16 kills, all 16 surviving mutants are loop-guard/body RELATIONAL ops (never reached). Confirmed vacuous: added `ensures(1==0)` → SUCCESS at depth 200, but postcondition.3 FAILS at depth 600 (body finally reached). Net 0/16 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.

`CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts(ll_counts, d_counts, ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lstart, lend)` is the HEAVIEST count-table case: its `if` branch delegates to `CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall` (so needs the full lz77+litlens+dists+forall-bounds harness) AND its `else` branch sums over four full histogram tables (so needs is_fresh on ll_counts/d_counts/ll_lengths/d_lengths too) = 7 is_fresh objects. A sound strong contract (is_fresh all 7, lstart<=lend<=lz77->size, forall litlens<=258 & dists DEFLATE bounds, assigns(), ensures ret>=ll_lengths[256]) verifies at depth 200 → 0/21 kills, all 21 surviving mutants are the if-guard relational/arith ops at line 317 and the loop bodies (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via the documented `ensures(1==0)` trick: it still "verifies" (mutation testing only runs post-success). Net 0/21 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.

`TryOptimizeHuffmanForRle(lz77, lstart, lend, ll_counts, d_counts, ll_lengths, d_lengths)` is the same wall — it calls `CalculateTreeSize`, `CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts` (twice), `OptimizeHuffmanForRle`, `ZopfliCalculateBitLengths`, `PatchDistanceCodesForBuggyDecoders`, all contract-replaced, so its requires is the UNION of theirs: is_fresh on lz77 + nested litlens/dists + ll_counts/d_counts/ll_lengths/d_lengths (7 is_fresh objects), forall litlens<=258 & DEFLATE dist bounds, ll/d_lengths<=15, counts<2^(64-9), assigns(object_whole(ll_lengths),object_whole(d_lengths)), ensures ret>=26. Verifies at depth 200 → 0/9 kills; all 9 survivors are the line-1272 min-select comparison and the two `treesize+datasize` return sums (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via `ensures(ret>=100000000)` → still SUCCESS at depth 200. Two extra gotchas while building it: (1) the quantified `requires` deref `litlens[qi]` generates a well-definedness BOUNDS assertion at the *caller* even though it's only assumed in the callee — guard the quantifier with `qi < lz77->size`; (2) unconstrained `lz77->size` makes the is_fresh byte-count `size * sizeof(*litlens)` overflow size_t (counterexample: size≈2^63), so add `requires(lz77->size <= ((size_t)-1)/sizeof(*lz77->litlens))`. Net 0/9 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.

`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt(lz77, lpos, ll_counts, d_counts)` is the same wall — it needs 8 is_fresh objects: `lz77` struct + its nested `ll_counts`/`d_counts`/`ll_symbol`/`d_symbol`/`dists` arrays + the two output tables `ll_counts`/`d_counts`. A sound strong contract verifies at depth 200 → 0/41 kills, all 41 survivors are the cumulative-index arithmetic (`llpos+i`, `lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`) and the decrement loops (never reached). Confirmed vacuous via the `ensures(1==0)` trick → still SUCCESS at depth 200. Two sizing details worth keeping: the cumulative arrays are indexed only up to the end of lpos's chunk, so size them exactly `(ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*(lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)` / `(ZOPFLI_NUM_D*(lpos/ZOPFLI_NUM_D)+ZOPFLI_NUM_D)` elements; and the symbol arrays are read at indices `lz77->ll_symbol[i]`/`d_symbol[i]` used to index the output tables, so a `forall (lpos<qi<size) ==> symbol[qi] < ZOPFLI_NUM_*` requires-bound is needed (guard the quantifier with `qi<size`). Net 0/41 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place.

`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram(lz77, lstart, lend, ll_counts, d_counts)` (the caller of `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt`) is the same wall — 8 is_fresh objects (lz77 struct + nested ll_counts/d_counts/ll_symbol/d_symbol/dists + the two output tables). Its small-range branch counts symbols directly; its large-range branch calls the contract-replaced `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt` (whose contract has NO ensures, so the output is fully havoc'd → no output postcondition can hold there anyway). Sound strong contract: is_fresh all 8 (size the cumulative arrays `ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*(size/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` so they cover every lpos<=size-1 without size-1 underflow when size=0), `lstart<=lend<=lz77->size`, overflow guard `size <= ((size_t)-1)/sizeof(*ll_counts) - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`, `forall qi<size ==> ll_symbol[qi]<ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` and same for d_symbol (full-range, since the callee needs (lpos,size)), assigns(object_whole(ll_counts/d_counts)). Verifies at depth 200 → 0/29 kills; all 29 survivors are the branch-guard (line 1371), the direct-count loop guard/dist-test (1375/1378), the `lstart>0` guard (1387), the subtraction-loop guards (1393/1397) and the `lend-1`/`lstart-1` callee args (1386/1391). Confirmed vacuous via the documented probe: `ensures((lstart+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3>lend) ==> d_counts[0]==999999)` (a false branch-1 claim) still SUCCEEDS at depth 200. Also note loop contracts are USELESS here: the avocado pipeline runs `goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5` WITHOUT `--apply-loop-contracts`, so `__CPROVER_loop_invariant`/loop-`assigns` annotations are silently ignored (loops are just unrolled 5x). Net 0/29 tooling-forced; left the strong sound spec in place. See also [[cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation]].

Bonus: at depth >=350, CBMC finds a real latent bug — `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` (macro in zopfli.h) writes through `realloc`'s return with no NULL check. See [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] and [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]].

1199	static double TryOptimizeHuffmanForRle(
1200	    const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77, size_t lstart, size_t lend,
1201	    const size_t *ll_counts, const size_t *d_counts,
1202	    unsigned *ll_lengths, unsigned *d_lengths)
1203	// Computes the current tree+data size, then re-derives bit lengths from the
1204	// RLE-optimized counts and, if that yields a smaller total, copies the new
1205	// lengths back into ll_lengths/d_lengths. The preconditions are the union of
1206	// what the callees need: a fresh, valid lz77 block (CalculateBlockSymbolSize-
1207	// GivenCounts), full-size count/length tables (CalculateTreeSize wants every
1208	// length <= 15), and counts small enough that ZopfliCalculateBitLengths can run
1209	// (each strictly below 2^(64-9)). Both candidate sizes are a CalculateTreeSize
1210	// result (>= 26) plus a non-negative data size, so the returned minimum is also
1211	// at least 26.
1212	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
1213	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend && lend <= lz77->size)
1214	/* Bound size so the is_fresh byte-counts below cannot overflow size_t; with no
1215	   overflow each index < lend <= size is provably within the fresh region. */
1216	__CPROVER_requires(lz77->size <= ((size_t)-1) / sizeof(*lz77->litlens))
1217	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
1218	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
1219	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1220	    size_t qi; (lstart <= qi && qi < lend && qi < lz77->size) ==> lz77->litlens[qi] <= 258
1221	})
1222	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1223	    size_t qj; (lstart <= qj && qj < lend && qj < lz77->size) ==>
1224	        (lz77->dists[qj] == 0 ||
1225	         (lz77->litlens[qj] >= 3 && lz77->dists[qj] <= 32768))
1226	})
1227	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_counts)))
1228	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_counts)))
1229	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))
1230	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_lengths)))
1231	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1232	    size_t ka; (ka < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> ll_lengths[ka] <= 15
1233	})
1234	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1235	    size_t kb; (kb < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> d_lengths[kb] <= 15
1236	})
1237	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1238	    size_t kc; (kc < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> ll_counts[kc] < ((size_t)1 << (sizeof(size_t) * CHAR_BIT - 9))
1239	})
1240	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1241	    size_t kd; (kd < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> d_counts[kd] < ((size_t)1 << (sizeof(size_t) * CHAR_BIT - 9))
1242	})
1243	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(ll_lengths))
1244	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(d_lengths))
1245	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= 26)
1246	{
1247	    size_t ll_counts2[ZOPFLI_NUM_LL];
1248	    size_t d_counts2[ZOPFLI_NUM_D];

288	
289	/*
290	Same as CalculateBlockSymbolSize, but with the histogram provided by the caller.
291	*/
292	static size_t CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts(const size_t *ll_counts,
293	                                                  const size_t *d_counts,
294	                                                  const unsigned *ll_lengths,
295	                                                  const unsigned *d_lengths,
296	                                                  const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
297	                                                  size_t lstart, size_t lend)
298	// The `else` branch sums ll_lengths[i]*ll_counts[i] over i in [0,256)U[257,286)
299	// plus the distance contributions over [0,30), then adds the end symbol
300	// ll_lengths[256]; so all four histogram tables must be full-size and valid.
301	// The `if` branch delegates to CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall, which needs a
302	// fresh lz77 with valid litlens/dists and the DEFLATE bounds on them. In both
303	// branches the running sum is non-negative and the end symbol ll_lengths[256]
304	// is always included, so the result is at least ll_lengths[256].
305	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_counts)))
306	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_counts)))
307	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))
308	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_lengths)))
309	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
310	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend && lend <= lz77->size)
311	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
312	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
313	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
314	    size_t qi; (lstart <= qi && qi < lend) ==> lz77->litlens[qi] <= 258
315	})

Verified. CBMC reports success, and mutation testing finds **no mutable operators** in GetDynamicLengths — its body is straight-line callee calls plus `ll_counts[256] = 1`, so there are no mutants to kill (kill score is moot, not a sign of spec weakness).

The contract I inserted is the strong, sound union of all four contract-replaced callees' preconditions:
- **`ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram`**: fresh `lz77` + nested `ll_counts`/`d_counts`/`ll_symbol`/`d_symbol`/`dists`, the cumulative-array sizing, the size overflow guard, and the `< ZOPFLI_NUM_*` symbol bounds.
- **`TryOptimizeHuffmanForRle`**: fresh `litlens`/`dists` with the DEFLATE bounds (`litlens<=258`, dist either 0 or `litlens>=3 && dist<=32768`).
- **`ZopfliCalculateBitLengths`/`PatchDistanceCodesForBuggyDecoders`**: full-size fresh `ll_lengths`/`d_lengths`.
- Postcondition `__CPROVER_return_value >= 26` (it returns `TryOptimizeHuffmanForRle`'s result), with `assigns` on both output tables.

lines: `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram(lz77, lstart, lend, ll_counts, d_counts)` (the caller of `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt`) is the same wall — 8 is_fresh objects (lz77 struct + nested ll_counts/d_counts/ll_symbol/d_symbol/dists + the two output tables). Its small-range branch counts symbols directly; its large-range branch calls the contract-replaced `ZopfliLZ77GetHistogramAt` (whose contract has NO ensures, so the output is fully havoc'd → no output postcondition can hold there anyway). Sound strong contract: is_fresh all 8 (size the cumulative arrays `ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*(size/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` so they cover every lpos<=size-1 without size-1 underflow when size=0), `lstart<=lend<=lz77->size`, overflow guard `size <= ((size_t)-1)/sizeof(*ll_counts) - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL`, `forall qi<size ==> ll_symbol[qi]<ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` and same for d_symbol (full-range, since the callee needs (lpos,size)), assigns(object_whole(ll_counts/d_counts)). Verifies at depth 200 → 0/29 kills; all 29 survivors are the branch-guard (line 1371), the direct-count loop guard/dist-test (1375/1378), the `lstart>0` guard (1387), the subtraction-loop guards (1393/1397) and the `lend-1`/`lstart-1` callee args (1386/1391). Confirmed vacuous via the documented probe: `ensures((lstart+ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3>lend) ==> d_counts[0]==999999)` (a false branch-1 claim) still SUCCEEDS at depth 200. Also note loop contracts are USELESS here: the avocado pipeline runs `goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5` WITHOUT `--apply-loop-contracts`, so `__CPROVER_loop_invariant`/loop-`assigns` annotations are silently ignored (loops are just unrolled 5x).
